scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (Punjab)
page_title: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (Punjab)

details: The "Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)" is a social welfare initiative by the Department of Social Security and Development of Women and Children, Government of Punjab. In this scheme, persons aged between 60 and 79 years receive a pension of ₹200/- per month, while those aged 80 years and above receive ₹500/- per month. The scheme applies only to persons belonging to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.
benefits: ₹200/- month, for persons aged 60 to 79 years.
₹500/- month, for persons aged 80 years and above.
eligibility: The applicant should be a resident of Punjab.
The applicant should be 60 years of age or older.
The applicant should be from a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.
The applicant should not be receiving pension benefits under any social security scheme.

online: The interested applicant should visit the District Social Security Officer and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the concerned staff.

In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the

Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

documents_required: Identity Proof (Voter ID Card, Driving License)

faqs.question: What is the age requirement to be eligible for the pension scheme under the 60 to 79 years category?
faqs.answer: Applicants must be aged between 60 and 79 years to receive a monthly pension of ₹200 under this scheme.

faqs.question: What is the pension amount for applicants aged 80 years and above?
faqs.answer: Applicants aged 80 years and above receive a higher pension amount of ₹500 per month under this scheme.

faqs.question: Do I need to be a resident of Punjab to apply for this pension scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, only residents of Punjab are eligible to apply for this pension scheme.

faqs.question: Is there an income or financial status requirement to qualify for this pension?
faqs.answer: Yes, applicants must belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family to be eligible for the pension.

faqs.question: Can I apply for this pension if I am already receiving benefits under another social security scheme?
faqs.answer: No, applicants should not be receiving pension benefits under any other social security scheme to qualify.

faqs.question: Where can I obtain the application form if I want to apply offline?
faqs.answer: You must visit the District Social Security Officer to request a hard copy of the prescribed application form.

faqs.question: What steps should I follow to submit my application offline?
faqs.answer: Fill the form, attach required documents, and submit it to the District Social Security Officer while ensuring you get an acknowledgement receipt.

faqs.question: What details should the acknowledgement receipt contain after submitting the application?
faqs.answer: The receipt should include the date and time of submission and a unique identification number, if applicable.

faqs.question: Where do I find the pension scheme application on the UMANG Portal?
faqs.answer: Navigate to the National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) page on UMANG and click "Apply Online."

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply for this pension scheme?
faqs.answer: You need identity proof, residence proof, age proof, BPL card, address proof, and bank account details.

faqs.question: Is a BPL card mandatory for applying to this pension scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, applicants must provide a Below Poverty Line (BPL) card as proof of eligibility.

faqs.question: Do I need to submit bank account details for this pension scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, you must provide your bank account details for pension disbursement.

faqs.question: Can I submit a driving license as identity proof for the application?
faqs.answer: Yes, a driving license is accepted as valid identity proof for the application.

faqs.question: Should I self-attest the documents before submitting them with the application?
faqs.answer: Yes, you must self-attest copies of all mandatory documents if required.
